title: Smoky Molten Topped German Sausage Platter
description: A hearty, smoky German sausage dish featuring a rich, molten topping served on a festive cedar plank. Perfect for a cold winter night or special occasion.

ingredients.name: Cedar Plank
ingredients.notes: soaked in water for at least 30 minutes before use

ingredients.name: Red Wine
ingredients.notes: deglazed to create the molten topping

ingredients.name: Bacon
ingredients.notes: crispy, chopped, and set aside for garnish

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: thinly sliced, caramelized in bacon drippings

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: minced, saut6d in butter until fragrant

instructions: Soak the cedar plank in water for at least 30 minutes before use.
Grill the sausages over medium-high heat until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the sausages to a baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until cooked through.
Meanwhile, caramelize the onions in a skillet over medium heat until deep golden brown, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and saut6 until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the red wine to the skillet and deglaze, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Bring the wine to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer until the liquid has reduced by half, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the mushroom duxelles and cook until heated through.
To assemble the platter, place the cooked sausages on the cedar plank, top with the molten onion and wine sauce, and garnish with crispy bacon, caramelized onions, and chopped parsley.
Serve immediately, allowing each guest to build their own sausage platter.
